entry.title: Bitcoin Core v31.1 Released
entry.summary:
Bitcoin Core version 31.1 has been officially released and is now available for download from [Bitcoin Core's official website](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-31.1/). This latest update brings several important improvements and changes including bug fixes, performance enhancements, and updated translations. Users are encouraged to report any issues via the project's GitHub issue tracker, accessible at [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues) and to join the announcement list through [Bitcoin Core's mailing list](https://bitcoincore.org/en/list/announcements/join/).
For those upgrading from an older version of Bitcoin Core, it is essential to shut down the existing application completely before initiating the upgrade process. This may take a few minutes depending on your system and setup. Installation procedures vary slightly by operating system: Windows users should run the installer, macOS users should copy over `/Applications/Bitcoin-Qt`, and Linux users should replace `bitcoind` or `bitcoin-qt`. It is possible to upgrade directly from a version that has reached its end-of-life (EOL), though this might require additional time if data directory migration is necessary.
Bitcoin Core 31.1 supports various operating systems including Linux Kernel 3.17 or newer, macOS 14 or newer, and Windows 10 (version 1903) or newer. While Bitcoin Core might operate on other Unix-like systems, these environments are less frequently tested and using the software on unsupported systems is not recommended.
Significant technical enhancements have also been included in this release. Notably, an issue with the chainstate database causing excessive disk reads and writes during normal operations has been resolved. In terms of privacy enhancements, a fix has been implemented to prevent IP address leaks when using the -privatebroadcast feature under specific circumstances. Additionally, numerous updates across system components like P2P networking, wallet management, and build processes improve overall stability and security. Specific changes include adjustments in transaction data handling, compaction policies in LevelDB, and modifications to networking behavior for private broadcasts.
This update also includes critical fixes in testing frameworks, documentation updates, continuous integration (CI) improvements, and miscellaneous code enhancements aimed at refining the user experience and developer engagement. The community owes thanks to a wide array of contributors who played a pivotal role in advancing this release, ensuring both the robustness and reliability of the Bitcoin Core software.
